channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Final AP mock NFL draft: Ward, Hunter, Carter go 1-2-3 and Saints trade up for Sanders
item.description: The Tennessee Titans explored all their options with the No. 1 pick in the NFL draft. They can’t pass up a shot at a potential franchise quarterback. If the Titans were going to trade the pick for more draft assets, a deal would’ve happened already.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Yankees' Jazz Chisholm Jr. violates MLB social media policy in post ripping umpire after ejection
item.description: New York Yankees infielder Jazz Chisholm Jr. was ejected from Thursday night's game and may have violated MLB policy with his public criticism on social media.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Real Salt Lake hosts Toronto FC with new era potentially dawning
item.description: Saturday night at America First Field might be about more than just the soccer. As Real Salt Lake gets set to host Toronto FC, all eyes will be on the field — and possibly the owner’s box — as signs point to a new chapter beginning for the club.
item.dc:creator: Mattey Casey, ABC 4

item.title: ESPN staple rips 'toxic' Nico Iamaleava, pleads with school to not take him
item.description: In another appearance on ESPN 'Around the Horn,' Bill Plaschke ripped Nico Iamaleave as "toxic" and begged UCLA not to take him from the portal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kawhi Leonard is fresh in mind and body as the streaking Los Angeles Clippers open the playoffs
item.description: INGLEWOOD, Calif. (AP) — Kawhi Leonard is fresh in mind and body this year, no longer relegated to watching from the sideline when the Los Angeles Clippers begin the postseason.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Red Rocks move on to National Finals for 5th straight year
item.description: FORT WORTH, Tex. (ABC4 Sports) - For the fifth straight year, the Utah gymnastics team will compete for the national championship. The Red Rocks won their semifinal meet over UCLA, LSU and Michigan State Thursday night with a score of 197.7625.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Chisholm ejected for arguing called 3rd strike during Yankees' 6-3 win over Rays
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Jazz Chisholm Jr. was ejected from the New York Yankees' 6-3 victory over the Tampa Bay Rays on Thursday night, then appeared to violate Major League Baseball's social media policy when he posted in-game criticism online.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yan Liu, Ashleigh Buhai share lead at 63 in the LPGA Tour's JM Eagle LA Championship
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Yan Liu birdied the final three holes in chilly conditions Thursday afternoon for a 9-under 63 and a share of the first-round lead with Ashleigh Buhai in the LPGA Tour's JM Eagle LA Championship.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: MLB Commissioner Rob Manfred meets with President Donald Trump at White House
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Baseball Commissioner Rob Manfred met this week at the White House with President Donald Trump. “President Trump is a longtime fan of baseball," MLB said in a statement Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Aaron Rodgers says he's not holding teams hostage as he ponders his future in the NFL
item.description: Aaron Rodgers is in no rush to make a decision about his future. The free-agent quarterback said during an appearance on “The Pat McAfee Show” on Thursday that he is “not holding anybody hostage" as he weighs whether to return for a 21st season. Rodgers was released by the New York Jets in March.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man United seals spectacular comeback to beat Lyon 5-4 and advance to Europa League semifinals
item.description: MANCHESTER, England (AP) — Manchester United’s season isn’t done yet. On a night of high drama at Old Trafford, the troubled Premier League club put its domestic problems to one side by sealing a spectacular comeback to beat Lyon 5-4 and advance to the semifinals of the Europa League.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: PGA star signs woman's leg after hitting it with errant tee shot
item.description: Billy Horschel found himself in an awkward moment Thursday at the RBC Heritage. He signed a woman's leg after he hit it with a tee shot.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tyler Herro scores 38 as Heat eliminate Bulls in Eastern Conference play-in round for 3rd straight year
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Once again, the Bulls' quest to reach the NBA's 16-team playoff field ended at the hands of the Miami Heat.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Inside Jackalopes' new identity: Humpback Chubs
item.description: On Wednesdays this summer, the Jackalopes will be the Grand Junction Humpback Chubs. A new identity for Grand Junction’s baseball team is here.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Cubs bullpen works 5 shutout innings, Hoerner's go-ahead triple in 10th hands Padres first home loss, 2-1
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — The San Diego Padres hadn't loss a home game yet on the young season. Until Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Warriors edge Grizzlies in Play-In Tournament thriller, clinch playoff spot
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The Golden State Warriors are officially going to the 2025 NBA Playoffs after a nail-biting 119-116 win over the Memphis Grizzlies on Tuesday night in San Francisco. The victory sets Golden State (48-34) up with a first-round matchup against the No.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Professional soccer could be headed to the North Bay
item.description: (KRON) -- The next professional sports team in the Bay Area could be bound for the North Bay. The City of Santa Rosa announced it has entered into exclusive negotiations with the United Soccer League (USL) to explore the possibilities of bringing both a men's team and a women's team to the city.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Cubs can't hold lead, MLB-best Padres surge late to win opening game of 3-game series in San Diego
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — The Cubs' West Coast gauntlet against two of the best teams in baseball rolls on this week, with two games left.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Chicago Sky select Ajsa Sivka, Hailey Van Lith in WNBA Draft
item.description: The Chicago Sky selected Slovenian stretch four Ajsa Sivka and TCU point guard Hailey Van Lith with their top two picks in the WNBA draft to address their need for perimeter shooting and to bolster their rebounding efforts.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Jazz close out season with 116-105 loss at Minnesota
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (ABC4 Sports) - The NBA season has mercifully come to an end for the Utah Jazz. The Jazz ended the worst season in franchise history with a 116-105 loss to the Minnesota Timberwolves, who were led by Anthony Edwards with 43 points.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Report: Bears sign Kyler Gordon to record extension, make him highest-paid slot corner in NFL
item.description: The Chicago Bears have agreed to a three-year, $40 million contract extension with corner Kyler Gordon, making him one of the highest-paid corners in the NFL and keeping him in Chicago for at least the next four seasons.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Garrett Crochet dazzles in return to South Side, takes no-no into the 8th in Red Sox victory
item.description: Garrett Crochet returned to the South Side with a vengeance, throwing seven and one-third innings and striking out eleven, but the Red Sox won the game 3-1 to avoid a sweep.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Western Michigan wins its 1st Frozen Four hockey title, beating Boston University 6-2
item.description: Owen Michaels didn’t hesitate after watching his shot slide into the open net with more than two minutes left to essentially seal Western Michigan’s first Frozen Four title in its first championship game.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Connor stars as Jets clinch Central Division title with shootout victory over Blackhawks
item.description: Kyle Connor and Cole Perfetti scored in the shootout, and the Winnipeg Jets clinched the franchise’s first Central Division title and the No. 1 seed for the Western Conference playoffs with a wild 5-4 victory over the Chicago Blackhawks on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rowdies lose homecoming matchup, but stadium comeback brings hope
item.description: For the first time in seven months, Rowdies fans are finally back home at Al Lang Stadium after the waterfront venue was impacted during back-to-back hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rory McIlroy has masterful start at Augusta and closes in on career Grand Slam
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — The greatest start in the 91-year history of the Masters wasn't enough for Rory McIlroy. He delivered what looked to be a haymaker late Saturday afternoon, a 6-iron so majestic and pure that he walked 10 yards before he saw it land 6 feet away for eagle on the 15th hole.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Texas man suing NFL player over December shooting at car lot
item.description: A Texas man who says Titans cornerback L'Jarius Sneed or his personal assistant shot at him while he was sitting inside a Mercedes-Benz on a dealer lot in December is suing for at least $1 million.
item.dc:creator: Teresa M. Walker, NewsNation

item.title: Chicago Fire face Inter Miami in Eastern Conference action
item.description: Inter Miami CF (4-0-2, second in the Eastern Conference) vs. Chicago Fire (3-2-2, sixth in the Eastern Conference) Chicago; Sunday, 4:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
